issue: AR label drops forward/down at distance and angle, intermittently — pops back up after. Root cause: AR.js back-face pose ambiguity.
confirmed_facts: Only occurs at distance (not close up)
Only occurs at angled view (not top-down)
Drops FORWARD toward camera direction, not straight down
Root cause: AR.js randomly picks back-face pose solution — places marker at wrong world position (below table surface), label follows and appears to drop
Same marker can render correctly AND dropped simultaneously for different detections in the same frame
Not caused by user walking to back of marker — AR.js guesses wrong pose from the front

attempts.why: Fix A-Frame 1.7.0 tick order so pose-filter runs after arjs-anchor
attempts.result: Fixed re-detection bug but did not fix dropping

attempts.what: Ghost group — move marker children to scene-level THREE.Group on markerLost
attempts.why: Prevent wasVisible poisoning while showing hold-mode content

attempts.result: Did not fix dropping — dropping is position not rotation

attempts.why: More AR.js samples to average away bad poses
Suspected A-Frame 1.7.0 default look-controls rotating camera with phone movement
attempts.result: Did not fix dropping — wrong diagnosis

attempts.what: Add after:['pose-filter'] to up-track, billboard, dash-track
attempts.why: Ensure child components read pose-filter's smoothed marker transform not raw arjs-anchor values

attempts.what: Revert up-track to hysteresis marker-local Y (no worldToLocal)
attempts.why: worldToLocal was suspected to give wrong positions in 1.7.0 tick order
attempts.result: Made it worse — hysteresis uses marker-local Y which changes with camera angle

attempts.what: Remove all lerp from pose-filter — let arjs-anchor smoothCount=30 do everything
attempts.why: Two smoothers fighting each other causing oscillation

attempts.what: Restore da4e292 exact components — hysteresis up-track, full lookAt billboard, no after constraints
attempts.why: Last known working version before issues started

attempts.what: Change billboard from full lookAt to lookAt(0,0,0) hardcoded origin
attempts.why: cam.getWorldPosition() in A-Frame 1.7.0 might return wrong position causing label to face wrong direction
attempts.result: Partial — full lookAt causes pitch toward camera at angle, which looks like dropping

attempts.why: Hysteresis uses marker-local Y which is wrong at angle — worldToLocal always offsets in world Y
attempts.result: Correct approach for positioning but dropping still occurs

attempts.what: Restore yaw-only billboard using hardcoded camera origin (0,0,0)
attempts.why: Full lookAt pitches label toward camera at angle — yaw-only keeps label perfectly vertical
attempts.result: Correct for orientation but dropping still occurs at distance

attempts.why: Heavier smoothing at distance where AR.js depth estimation is worse
attempts.result: User confirmed not a lerp issue — did not fix

attempts.what: Lock position on markerFound — freeze pose, only billboard updates
attempts.why: Ignore all per-frame AR.js noise after initial detection
attempts.result: Label completely frozen — unacceptable UX, reverted

attempts.what: Two-phase lerp: alpha=0.3 first 20 frames, alpha=0.05 steady state + quat rejection
attempts.why: Fast initial lock then very slow steady tracking to resist noise
attempts.result: User confirmed not a lerp issue

attempts.why: Test pure AR.js output to isolate whether smoothing is involved
attempts.result: Reverted — confirmed not a lerp issue

attempts.why: Higher resolution gives AR.js finer pixel accuracy on marker corners, improving 3D depth calculation especially for edge-of-frame markers

attempts.what: Back-face pose rejection — check marker normal dot product with camera direction, freeze to last good pose
attempts.why: AR.js pose ambiguity: two valid poses exist for a flat marker. Back-face detection places marker at wrong world Y, so label drops. Reject frames where marker normal points away from camera.
attempts.result: Fixed mirroring but caused label dropping on its own — unacceptable, reverted

attempts.what: Zero marker rotation every frame in pose-filter tick: obj.quaternion.set(0,0,0,1)
attempts.why: Discard AR.js detected rotation entirely — use marker position only. Back-face vs front-face rotation ambiguity becomes irrelevant. Billboard (yaw-only) handles facing camera.
attempts.result: FIXED. No dropping observed. Labels correctly face camera regardless of AR.js pose solution. Commit 6e0003b.

current_state.pose_filter: visibility only + zero rotation every frame (obj.quaternion.set(0,0,0,1))

open_issue.description: Intermittent label tilt — labels occasionally pitch backward/downward for a frame
open_issue.hypothesis: AR.js position estimate is noisy frame-to-frame. Yaw-only billboard helps but position spikes still cause visible lurches.
open_issue.next: Position smoothing on marker detected position
